what_was_done: Evaluated Angular Material vs Astro+Svelte for public-facing UI — chose Astro (SEO/static) + Svelte (interactive islands) + Tailwind CSS
Implemented two API fixes to align slot lock behavior with business intent
Built the complete public landing page: therapist bio, services, slot browser, appointment request form, thank-you screen
Added Astro dev server to Docker Compose stack
All 473 API tests pass (317 unit + 156 integration)
key_decisions_made: Astro chosen over Angular for public pages — SEO performance, static content for therapist bio/services, Svelte islands for interactivity
Svelte 5 chosen for interactive islands — lightest-weight option with first-class Astro support, better than Angular (experimental Astro support)
Tailwind CSS for styling — shared tokens between Astro static components and Svelte islands
Slot locking is TRANSPARENT to visitors — no countdown timer, no expiry warnings. Lock is a silent UX optimization to reduce therapist's manual conflict resolution
Multiple REQUESTED appointments for the same slot are allowed by design — therapist resolves conflicts manually during confirmation
Locked slots are filtered from the available-slots API response — when Visitor1 locks a slot, Visitor2 won't see it on next refresh
The Angular Material discussion was for the THERAPIST DASHBOARD (future work), not the public pages

fixes.name: Fix 1: Filter locked slots from available-slots response
fixes.reason: GetAvailableSlotsHandler was passing an empty activeLocks collection to AvailabilityComputer, so locked slots remained visible to all visitors. The code comment said 'lock-level filtering happens on the frontend' but the user wants the API to handle this.

files_modified.change: Implemented findActiveByDateRange — DQL query: startTime < to AND endTime > from AND expiresAt > NOW()
Replaced empty ArrayCollection() with $this->slotLockRepository->findActiveByDateRange($from, $to). Removed unused ArrayCollection import.
Added slotLockRepository->findActiveByDateRange mock stub returning empty ArrayCollection in both test methods

fixes.name: Fix 2: Allow multiple REQUESTED appointments for same slot
fixes.reason: AppointmentRequestService.verifySlotAvailable() used findBlockingByDateRange() which returns REQUESTED+CONFIRMED appointments. This blocked a second REQUESTED appointment — contradicting the design intent. Only CONFIRMED appointments should block new requests.

files_modified.change: Implemented findConfirmedByDateRange — DQL query: status = CONFIRMED AND startTime < to AND endTime > from
Changed verifySlotAvailable() to use findConfirmedByDateRange() instead of findBlockingByDateRange(). Updated design comment.
Replaced all findBlockingByDateRange mock stubs with findConfirmedByDateRange

api_changes.important_semantic_split: The slot browser (GetAvailableSlotsHandler) blocks on REQUESTED+CONFIRMED appointments. The appointment request validation (AppointmentRequestService) blocks on CONFIRMED only. This is intentional — visitors see slots with pending requests as unavailable, but the submission endpoint allows duplicate REQUESTED appointments.

tech_stack.build_output: Static HTML + 8.56 KB gzipped Svelte island JS

config.purpose: Astro config: svelte + tailwind integrations, output=static
Custom brand green color palette (50-900) + Inter font family

pages_and_layouts.purpose: Single landing page: TherapistBio + ServicesList + AppointmentFlow (client:visible)
HTML shell: lang=es, Inter font from Google Fonts, meta tags, Tailwind base styles

content_collections.purpose: Astro content collection schema for 'site' (optional: title, name, credentials, photo)
Therapist bio: name='Dra. María González', credentials, photo path, markdown body
Services list: 8 therapy specialties as markdown bullets

static_components.purpose: Reusable h2 heading with optional subtitle
Reads therapist content entry, renders photo/name/credentials/bio
Reads services content entry, renders list with custom bullet styling

svelte_islands.purpose: State machine orchestrator — owns 3-step flow: browsing → filling_form → success
Week grid with 7 date columns, modality toggle, prev/next nav, 30s auto-refresh, lock-on-click
Single clickable time slot button with loading spinner during lock
Three-way pill toggle: Todas / Online / Presencial
Intake form (5 fields), slot header display, per-field validation errors, INVALID_LOCK_TOKEN retry logic
Success screen with appointment summary and 'book another' button

services_and_types.purpose: Centralized API layer: apiRequest<T> wrapper, fetchAvailableSlots, lockSlot, requestAppointment
TypeScript interfaces: SlotData, SlotsResponse, LockResponse, AppointmentResponse, ApiError class, Modality types
Date helpers: getWeekStart/End, formatDate/Time, toDateParam, getWeekDates, getWeekdayName (locale=es-VE)

filling_form.data: slot: SlotData, modality: Modality, lockData: LockResponse | null

transitions.browsing_to_filling_form: User clicks slot → lock attempt → store lockData (or null if lock failed) → transition
transitions.filling_form_to_browsing: User clicks back OR 409 SLOT_NOT_AVAILABLE on submit OR 400 INVALID_LOCK_TOKEN retry fails

error_handling.409_SLOT_NOT_AVAILABLE_on_lock: Slot greyed out, refresh slots list, show error banner
error_handling.409_SLOT_NOT_AVAILABLE_on_submit: Return to browser with error message
error_handling.400_INVALID_LOCK_TOKEN_on_submit: Silently retry submission WITHOUT lock_token (lock expired but slot may still be free)
error_handling.422_VALIDATION_ERROR: Per-field inline error messages from error.details

slot_locking_behavior.user_experience: Completely transparent — visitor never sees lock status, countdowns, or expiry warnings
slot_locking_behavior.on_click: Silent POST to lock-slot. If 201: store lock_token, proceed to form. If 409: slot taken, refresh and let user pick another. If other error: proceed without lock.
slot_locking_behavior.on_submit: lock_token passed if available. If INVALID_LOCK_TOKEN: retry without it. If slot truly taken: back to browser.
slot_locking_behavior.design_rationale: Lock reduces duplicate requests (less manual work for therapist) but is optional. Two visitors CAN submit for the same slot — therapist resolves manually.

docker_changes.volume_added: astro_node_modules (named volume to avoid Windows/Linux node_modules conflicts)
docker_changes.cors_note: API's APP_FRONTEND_URL env var must include http://localhost:4321 for local dev

slot_lock_semantics.case_1: Visitor1 selects a slot → lock acquired → Visitor2's next refresh of available-slots won't show that slot (API filters locked slots)
slot_lock_semantics.case_2: If both visitors somehow submit for the same slot (race condition or no locks used), both get 201 REQUESTED. Therapist resolves the conflict manually on their dashboard.
slot_lock_semantics.lock_is_optional: Appointments can be submitted without a lock_token. The lock is purely a UX optimization to reduce therapist workload.
slot_lock_semantics.lock_ttl: 600 seconds (10 min) by default. Expired locks are cleaned by app:cleanup-slot-locks cron command.

appointment_blocking.public_available_slots: REQUESTED + CONFIRMED + active locks all block (slot won't appear)
appointment_blocking.appointment_request_submission: Only CONFIRMED blocks. Multiple REQUESTED appointments for the same slot are permitted.

business_rules_clarified.no_countdown_timer: The user explicitly rejected showing lock expiry countdown to visitors. The lock is invisible to them.

remaining_work.angular_material_consideration: Angular Material was discussed as a good fit for the therapist dashboard (forms, tables, navigation, dialogs). The public pages use Astro+Svelte instead. The dashboard could be a separate Angular app or integrated differently — this decision is still open.

known_issues: UI/CLAUDE.md is outdated — says 'TBD' for framework, styling, state management. Needs updating to reflect Astro+Svelte+Tailwind.
Therapist photo referenced as /photos/therapist.jpg in content but the file doesn't exist in UI/public/photos/
No linting, formatting, or testing setup in the UI project
UI/src/utils/validation.ts was planned in the project structure but was never created (client-side validation not implemented)
UI/public/favicon.svg and UI/public/og-image.jpg were planned but not created
The ServicesList component uses a ✦ emoji as bullet points — may need to verify cross-browser rendering
Rate limit awareness: API allows 10 req/min on public endpoints. The 30s auto-refresh is well within limits but rapid modality toggling could theoretically hit it — no debounce implemented

public_slots.params: from (YYYY-MM-DD, required), to (YYYY-MM-DD, required), modality (ONLINE|IN_PERSON, optional)
public_slots.response: { success: true, data: { from, to, modality, slots_by_date: { 'YYYY-MM-DD': [{ start_time, end_time, duration_minutes }] }, total_slots } }

lock_slot.response_201: { success: true, data: { lock_token, slot_start_time, slot_end_time, expires_at } }

request_appointment.body: { slot_start_time, modality, full_name, phone, email, city, country, lock_token? }
request_appointment.response_201: { success: true, data: { appointment: { id, start_time, end_time, modality, status, created_at }, message } }
